summary: The Agent concluded that while lymphoid tissues grow substantially in youth, reaching up to about twice their original volume but not necessarily peaking at exactly doubled sizes around age thirteen; this description does not accurately reflect standard developmental trends based on referenced studies which show earlier peak increases without consistent post-age-thirteen reduction phases for all such tissues collectively mentioned under 'Scammons' Growth Curve'. Therefore, it identified an overstatement leading directly towards classifying choice B incorrectly per given criteria against factual data presented here today concerning human development milestones
